mechanism: These represent the two horns of the fiscal dominance trap paradox. The doom loop is the tail risk from fiscal inaction (deficits spiral), while the consolidation threshold marks where correction itself becomes dangerous (private-sector recession). Together they define the narrow and potentially non-existent corridor of safe fiscal adjustment.
regime_dependence: Always present under fiscal dominance; relative salience depends on whether deficit is expanding (doom loop risk rises) or contracting (consolidation risk rises). Both intensify at higher debt/GDP.
